results.question: How does the Tsallis-Pareto distribution handle high-pT tails compared to standard Boltzmann-Juttner?
results.answer_preview:
> "The identified particle spectra at RHIC and LHC do not follow Boltzmann-Gibbs distribution due to the possible QCD contributions at high-pT." — Khuntia_2019_1808.02383.pdf, Introduction [[1]]()

> "To describe the complete transverse spectra of identified particles, one has to account for the power-law contribution at high-pT  and this empirically takes care for the possible QCD contributions."
results.answer:
> "The identified particle spectra at RHIC and LHC do not follow Boltzmann-Gibbs distribution due to the possible QCD contributions at high-pT." — Khuntia_2019_1808.02383.pdf, Introduction [[1]]()

> "To describe the complete transverse spectra of identified particles, one has to account for the power-law contribution at high-pT  and this empirically takes care for the possible QCD contributions." — Khuntia_2019_1808.02383.pdf, Introduction [[1]]()

> "The Tsallis non-extensive distribution function accounts for the power-law contribution at high-pT and this could be understood as empirically taking of QCD contributions." — Rath_2020_1908.04208.pdf, Section II.A [[2]]()

> "The use of Tsallis non-extensive statistics incorporates a power-law distribution, which has a dominant contribution at higher collision energies, making a better description of the transverse momentum spectra of charged particles and identified particles up to very high values of pT ." — Rath_2020_1908.04208.pdf, Introduction [[2]]()

> "It is found that the Tsallis distribution generally leads to a better description for the complete transverse momentum spectra whereas the BGBW model explains the bulk part of the system." — Rath_2020_1908.04208.pdf, Abstract [[2]]()

> "Conclusively, the BGBW explains the bulk part of the transverse momentum spectra and the description is better for the higher multiplicity classes, whereas the non-extensive Tsallis statistics describes the charged particle transverse momentum for the complete pT-range for the low multiplicity classes." — Rath_2020_1908.04208.pdf, Summary [[3]]()

> "The resultant expression acts as an exponential and a power-law function for small and large pT, respectively." — Khuntia_2019_1808.02383.pdf, Introduction [[1]]() (referring to the Hagedorn function which relates to Tsallis via $n = \frac{1}{q-1}$)

> "In the limit of $q \to 1$, Tsallis distribution (Eq. 3) reduces to the standard Boltzmann-Gibbs distribution (Eq. 1)." — Khuntia_2019_1808.02383.pdf, Introduction [[1]]()

results.question: Does the manuscript compare the boson probability distribution model against a Tsallis or Blast-Wave baseline? If so, what is the stated motivation?
results.answer_preview: > "[In the present work, we study the recent collision energy and multiplicity dependence of the charged particle transverse momentum spectra as measured by the ALICE collaboration in pp collisions at the Large Hadron Collider using the non-extensive Tsallis distribution and the Boltzmann-Gibbs (BGBW) model. A thermodynamically consistent form of the Tsallis distribution is used to extract the kin
results.answer:
> "[In the present work, we study the recent collision energy and multiplicity dependence of the charged particle transverse momentum spectra as measured by the ALICE collaboration in pp collisions at the Large Hadron Collider using the non-extensive Tsallis distribution and the Boltzmann-Gibbs (BGBW) model. A thermodynamically consistent form of the Tsallis distribution is used to extract the kinetic freeze-out parameters from the transverse momentum spectra of charged particles at mid-rapidity.]"

The manuscript compares the boson probability distribution model against both a Tsallis and Blast-Wave baseline [[1]](). The stated motivation for using these models is to study the recent collision energy and multiplicity dependence of the charged particle transverse momentum spectra in pp collisions at the Large Hadron Collider, specifically focusing on extracting kinetic freeze-out parameters from the transverse momentum spectra.

[[1]]() Rath_2020_1908.04208.pdf
